title: ACB system down affecting zone 5 pairing at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet)

site.notes: Case metadata consistently references UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet). Supporting work-order evidence also references Fort Worth/Haslet hub, though OCR is noisy.

kpi_tracking.basis: Tracked from Salesforce case creation time to case resolved/closed time on the same date.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Case created at 7:36 PM for subject 'ACB System Down'.
Case status updated to Resolved and case closed at 9:20 PM.

notes: Elapsed time reflects case-open to case-resolved/closed duration, not independently verified production downtime.
Operational recovery was reported slightly before closure, with monitoring noted around 9:17 PM.

symptoms: Zone 5 unable to get a pair
After an AMR with low battery under the rack was replaced, every AMR reportedly went to the hospital
Zone 5 continued having issues until robot 158 was removed from the system
incident_description: A high-priority production-impacting malfunction was opened for UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) with subject 'ACB System Down.' Source text states the site reported zone 5 was unable to get a pair. Supporting case notes describe an AMR with low battery under the rack, a replacement attempt, and subsequent behavior where AMRs went to the hospital. Later support updates reported that removing robot 158 from the system corresponded with the system beginning to function again.
customer_operational_impact: Production was seriously impacted. The site reported zone 5 could not get a pair, indicating impaired material-handling operations in that area.
what_resolved_it: Source-supported resolution notes state the site removed robot 158 from the system; the robot was then on charger, and the system was reported to begin functioning again and remain operational during approximately 10 minutes of monitoring. A case summary also states robot 158 was shown as not mapped.
follow_up_required: SME review recommended to confirm whether case-open-to-close duration should be treated as downtime.
SME review recommended to confirm the source wording inconsistency between 'ACB' and 'ECB' in the case summary.
Review whether robot 158 mapping/state issue should be captured in downstream timeline or runbook candidate analysis without asserting root cause.
support_boundary: Support handled incident triage, escalation, case tracking, and validation of reported recovery. Site-side action and CBRE/work-order involvement are explicitly referenced, indicating part of the response depended on site/facilities processes rather than remote support alone.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Case 00228723 created 3/13/2026 7:36 PM; subject 'ACB System Down'; account 'UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet)'; priority '2 - High: Production seriously impacted'.
Description: zone 5 unable to get a pair; AMR had low battery under the rack; site replaced the AMR; every AMR goes to the hospital.
Case status changed from In Progress to Escalated at 8:30 PM.
Support update states site removed robot 158 from the system, it was on charger, and after removal the system began functioning again and remained operational during about 10 minutes of monitoring.
Case status updated from Escalated to Resolved and case closed at 9:20 PM.
Resolution details state site removed robot 158, which was shown to not be mapped, and the system was reported functional again.
Work-order evidence shows emergency/dispatched maintenance context for Haslet hub and references ACB repair in zone 5, though OCR is low quality.

conflicts_or_ambiguities: Case summary on page 12 references 'ACB System Down' but resolution text says 'ECB system was reported to be functional again.'
Case-open to case-close duration is source-supported, but exact production downtime is not independently validated.
Some supporting work-order and RMS screenshot OCR is noisy or garbled, limiting confidence in detailed field extraction.
unsupported_or_missing_details: No independently verified root cause is stated.
No exact direct telemetry proving full system health after recovery is provided.
No precise outage start before case creation is established.

retrieval_text: Case 00228723 for UPS Fort Worth TX Haslet. High-priority production-impacting ACB system down incident affecting zone 5 pairing. Site reported unable to get a pair, low-battery AMR under rack, replacement attempt, and AMRs going to hospital. Case escalated. Reported recovery occurred after site removed robot 158 from the system; robot 158 was noted as not mapped in case summary. System reportedly functioned again and remained operational during monitoring. Case opened 2026-03-13 7:36 PM and resolved/closed 9:20 PM. Haslet hub, Fort Worth, TX, RMS, AMR, CBRE work order.
